title: Cell-free biosynthesis and engineering of ribosomally synthesized lanthipeptides
objective: Develop UniBioCat, a unified biocatalysis system based on cell-free gene expression for rapid biosynthesis, characterization, and engineering of RiPPs, specifically focusing on lanthipeptide variants
problem_statement: A vast majority of RiPP gene clusters remain unexplored in microbial genomes due to lack of rapid and efficient heterologous expression systems. Current cell-based systems are time-consuming, require extensive molecular cloning, and suffer from precursor peptide insolubility, modification enzyme insolubility, and cytotoxicity of RiPPs to host cells. This creates a bottleneck in characterizing and synthesizing new RiPP products despite rapid identification of gene clusters

research_motivation.discovery_pace: Rate of new RiPP gene cluster identification greatly outpaces capacity to characterize and synthesize actual RiPP products
bottlenecks_in_traditional_systems: Time-consuming and laborious molecular cloning steps
Lengthy cell cultivation and protein purification procedures
Potential cytotoxicity of RiPPs to host cells
Need for individual in vitro reconstitution of enzymatic catalysis

unibiocat_system.name: UniBioCat (Unified Biocatalysis)
advantages_over_cell_based: Enables synthesis of complex natural products from DNA inputs
unibiocat_system.platform_basis: In vitro transcription and translation without intact living cells

demonstration_compound.application: Full biosynthetic pathway reconstitution for de novo biosynthesis

engineering_capabilities.deletion_strategy: Deleted several protease/peptidase genes from source strain
engineering_capabilities.outcome: Enhanced synthesis and screening of salivaricin B variants with enhanced antimicrobial activity

validation_approach.generalizability_demonstration: Synthesized and evaluated ten uncharacterized lanthipeptides
validation_approach.outcome: Confirmed UniBioCat platform generalizability across diverse RiPP substrates

lasso_peptide_mechanisms.application: Analyzed interactions between RiPP precursor recognition elements (RRE) and cognate lasso leader peptidase

pure_system_applications.technology: Reconstituted in vitro translation system (PURE system)
uses: Synthesize different types of RiPPs and analogs

ripp_expression_bottlenecks_addressed.cell_free_advantage: Overcomes all these limitations by providing controlled environment without cell toxicity constraints

expected_impact.acceleration: Accelerates discovery, characterization, and synthesis of RiPPs
applications: Unlock new RiPPs from growing genomic data repositories
expected_impact.platform_utility: Expected to serve as valuable platform for future RiPP research and development

research_gap_addressed.problem: Vast majority of RiPP gene clusters remain unexplored and uncharacterized
research_gap_addressed.solution: UniBioCat provides rapid, efficient, and generalizable system for synthesis and characterization
research_gap_addressed.significance: Bridges gap between gene cluster discovery and functional characterization
